meta.name: Asian Fusion Fried Rice
meta.subtitle: Wok-style fried rice with aromatics, egg, and a savory soy-sesame finish.

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Designed for SSA planners: fast weeknight, leftover-friendly, batch-friendly.
Uses day-old rice for best texture; includes fresh-rice quick-cool fallback.

summary: Fast, savory fried rice built on aromatics, egg, and a soy-sesame sauce. Perfect for leftovers and flexible add-ins.

yield.notes: About 4 bowls (1 to 1.5 cups each), depending on add-ins.

optional.label: Wok spatula (if using a wok)
optional.notes: Helps toss rice quickly without breaking grains.

notes: Use tamari for gluten-free soy sauce, and ensure oyster sauce (if used) is gluten-free.

ingredients.label: Cooked rice, chilled (preferably day-old)
notes: Jasmine rice is classic; medium-grain works too.
If using fresh rice, see Step 1 quick-cool method to prevent mushy fried rice.

ingredients.label: Green onions, sliced (whites and greens separated)
Fresh ginger, finely minced or grated
Soy sauce (or tamari)
notes: Start with 2 tbsp and adjust at the end to taste.

ingredients.label: Toasted sesame oil
notes: Add at the end for aroma; do not use as the main cooking oil.

ingredients.label: Sugar or honey
notes: Optional—balances salt/umami. Omit if you prefer.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Optional. Add only after tasting—soy sauce often supplies enough salt.

ingredients.label: Optional protein: cooked chicken, shrimp, tofu, or leftover meat (bite-size)
notes: If using raw shrimp, cook it first and remove from pan, then add back at the end.

ingredients.label: Optional: chili crisp or chili oil (for heat)

steps.title: Prep rice (best texture)
instructions: If using day-old rice: break up clumps with clean hands or a fork so grains separate.
If using freshly cooked rice: spread hot rice on a wide tray, drizzle 1 tsp neutral oil, and toss lightly. Refrigerate 10–15 minutes until surface is cool and drier to the touch.
checks: Rice grains should feel separate, not wet or sticky on the surface.

steps.title: Mix the sauce
instructions: In a small bowl, mix soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar (if using), black pepper, and oyster sauce (if using).
Set sesame oil aside to add at the end.

steps.title: Scramble the eggs
instructions: Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add 1 tsp neutral oil.
Pour in beaten eggs and scramble until just set (soft curds).

steps.title: Sauté aromatics and vegetables
instructions: Add remaining oil to the pan and keep heat at medium-high.
Add green onion whites, garlic, and ginger. Stir constantly for 20–30 seconds until fragrant.
Add frozen peas and stir-fry 1 minute to heat through.
checks: Aromatics should be fragrant, not browned/burnt.
Carrots should soften slightly but keep some bite.

steps.title: Fry the rice
instructions: Add the rice to the pan. Increase heat to high if your stove can handle it without smoking.
Press and spread rice across the pan so it contacts the hot surface; let it sit 30–45 seconds to dry and toast.
Toss/stir and repeat once more if you want more toasted flavor.
checks: Rice should start to smell nutty/toasty and look more separated.

steps.title: Season, add eggs (and protein), and finish
instructions: Pour the sauce mixture around the edges of the pan (it sizzles and reduces), then toss to coat rice evenly.
Add scrambled eggs back in. Add cooked protein (if using) and toss until hot.
Turn off heat. Drizzle toasted sesame oil and toss once more.
Taste and adjust: add a splash more soy sauce for salt, a touch more vinegar for brightness, or a pinch of salt only if needed.
Top with green onion greens and sesame seeds (optional). Serve immediately.
checks: Rice should be evenly seasoned, not wet/saucy.
Egg should be distributed in small curds.

notes: Cool quickly, store in a shallow container.
Reheat hot (165°F / 74°C) for best safety and texture.
Freeze in flat bags for quick thawing; reheat directly from frozen in a covered skillet with a splash of water.

reheat.skillet: Medium-high with 1 tsp oil; spread rice and let steam 1 minute, then toss until hot.
reheat.microwave: Cover with a damp paper towel; heat in 60-second bursts, stirring between.

notes: Rice safety: cooked rice should be cooled quickly and refrigerated within 1 hour if possible.

variations.label: Garlic-Butter Fried Rice (fusion)
variations.notes: Swap 1 tbsp neutral oil for 1 tbsp butter; add at the end with sesame oil.

variations.label: Chili Crisp Fried Rice
variations.notes: Stir in 1–2 tsp chili crisp at the end; reduce soy sauce slightly to keep salt balanced.

variations.label: Vegetable-Loaded
variations.notes: Add diced bell pepper, corn, chopped cabbage, or mushrooms. Cook watery veg (mushrooms) before rice so the pan stays dry.

notes: Texture rule: keep the pan hot, keep the rice dry, and season at the edges of the pan.
If the rice gets wet: increase heat, spread it out, and cook 1–2 minutes to drive off moisture.
Optional protein guideline: add cooked protein after seasoning so it warms through without overcooking.
